etymology.description: 巴(巴) (bā, "to cling; to hope for") is a phonetic element here. 金(金) (jīn, "metal; gold") can symbolize wealth, value, or excellence, but in this case, it serves as a phonetic component. The name 巴金 (Bā Jīn) is a phonetic transcription rather than a semantic combination.
etymology.interpretation: 巴金 (Bā Jīn) is the pen name of a notable Chinese writer and anarchist, taking inspiration from Russian and French literature. The characters themselves in this context are used primarily for their pronunciation as a phonetic representation of his chosen pen name.

examples.chinese: 巴金是一位著名的中国作家(巴金是一位著名的中國作家)。
examples.pinyin: Bā Jīn shì yī wèi zhùmíng de Zhōngguó zuòjiā.
examples.english: Ba Jin was a famous Chinese writer.

examples.chinese: 我读了巴金的小说(我讀了巴金的小說)，深受感动(深受感動)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ dúle Bā Jīn de xiǎoshuō, shēn shòu gǎndòng.
examples.english: I read Ba Jin's novel and was deeply moved.

examples.chinese: 巴金的作品在文学界影响深远(巴金的作品在文學界影響深遠)。
examples.pinyin: Bā Jīn de zuòpǐn zài wénxué jiè yǐngxiǎng shēnyuǎn.
examples.english: Ba Jin's works have a profound impact in the literary world.
